title: Comprehensive App Review
description:
Deep review of the entire Myrivo codebase across 7 phases: Security, Data Model, Business Logic, Architecture, Frontend/UX, Testing, and Operations.

## Findings Convention

Each child task represents a review area. Findings are tracked as **comments** on the relevant task using this format:

**Severity:** `critical` | `high` | `medium` | `low` | `info`
**Category:** `security` | `bug` | `performance` | `correctness` | `maintainability` | `accessibility` | `gap`
**Location:** `file/path.ts:line`
**Finding:** Description of the issue
**Recommendation:** Suggested fix or next step

After each review area is complete, the task notes field gets a summary with counts by severity. Tasks are closed with a close_reason summarizing the outcome (e.g., "3 critical, 5 high, 2 medium findings").

## Phases

1. Security & Data Integrity (P0)
2. Data Model & Database (P1)
3. Business Logic (P1)
4. Architecture & Code Quality (P2)
5. Frontend & UX (P2)
6. Testing & Reliability (P2)
7. Operations & Deployment (P3)

title: Phase 1: Authentication & Authorization Review
description:
Review all auth and authorization paths for correctness and security.

## Scope
- Session validation in `lib/auth/authenticated-workspace.ts`
- Role/permission checks in `lib/auth/authorization.ts`
- Every API route and server action verifies auth
- Auth pages (login, signup, forgot/reset password)
- Supabase Auth integration correctness

## Acceptance Criteria
- All API routes confirmed to check authentication
- Role-based access verified for dashboard vs storefront vs platform routes
- No privilege escalation paths found or all documented
- Session handling is secure (expiry, refresh, invalidation)
notes: Review complete. 0 critical, 0 high, 1 medium, 4 low, 15 info. Auth posture is strong. All 90+ routes checked — consistent use of getUser() server-side validation, proper role hierarchy, universal CSRF protection. Main recommendation: add middleware-level route protection as defense-in-depth.

comments.author: mdbaldwin1
comments.text:
## Findings

### Finding 1
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/middleware.ts:1-53`
**Finding:** Next.js middleware does NOT enforce authentication on any route. It only handles cookie management and auth code redirects. All auth is deferred to individual API route handlers. A new route that forgets to add auth checks would be unprotected.
**Recommendation:** Add middleware-level session checks for `/dashboard/*` and `/account/*` as defense-in-depth. Redirect unauthenticated users to login.

### Finding 2
**Severity:** low
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/accessibility/reports/route.ts:24`
**Finding:** POST /api/accessibility/reports allows anonymous submissions using admin client (bypasses RLS). Rate limited at 6/min with origin checking.
**Recommendation:** Likely intentional — acceptable design with existing mitigations.

### Finding 3
**Severity:** low
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/reviews/route.ts:174`, `apps/web/app/api/reviews/media/upload-url/route.ts:31`
**Finding:** Review submission and media upload endpoints lack user auth — accept `reviewerEmail` from POST body. Media upload-url endpoint could be abused for storage spam.
**Recommendation:** By design (guest reviews) with solid mitigations (rate limits, fingerprinting, moderation queue). Consider CAPTCHA for unauthenticated submissions and monitoring media upload usage.

### Finding 4
**Severity:** low
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/storefront/cart-preview/route.ts:32`
**Finding:** Cart preview endpoint has no auth and no rate limiting. Uses admin client. Could be used for automated data scraping.
**Recommendation:** Add rate limiting to this endpoint.

### Finding 5
**Severity:** low
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/security/rate-limit.ts:12-19`
**Finding:** Rate limiting uses in-memory Map — won't work across multiple serverless instances. Relies on x-forwarded-for which is trustworthy on Vercel but could be spoofed elsewhere.
**Recommendation:** Document Vercel dependency. Consider external rate limiting if scaling beyond single-instance.

### Positive Findings (info)
- Every authenticated API route uses `supabase.auth.getUser()` (server-side validation, not just JWT)
- Three-tier authorization (store permissions, store roles, platform roles) consistently applied across 90+ routes
- All platform admin routes properly require "admin" for mutations, "support" for reads
- CSRF protection via `enforceTrustedOrigin()` applied universally to mutation endpoints
- Open redirect protection in `sanitizeReturnTo` is comprehensive (allowlist, protocol-relative URL rejection)
- Invite tokens hashed before storage, validated via hash comparison
- No server actions in codebase — all mutations through API routes
- Customer routes properly scope data access by authenticated user's email

## Summary: 0 critical, 0 high, 1 medium, 4 low, 15 info

title: Phase 3: Order Lifecycle & Fulfillment Review
description:
Review order states, fulfillment flow, and shipping integration.

## Scope
- Order status state machine and valid transitions
- Fulfillment workflow (packing, shipping, delivered)
- Shipping label generation via EasyPost
- Tracking webhook updates
- Refund handling (noted as a known gap)
- Order export functionality

## Acceptance Criteria
- State machine transitions are enforced (no invalid jumps)
- Shipping integration handles all EasyPost webhook events
- Refund gap assessed and documented
- Order export is complete and accurate
notes: Review complete. 0 critical, 2 high, 6 medium, 3 low, 1 info. HIGH: cancelled orders can be resurrected (no terminal state enforcement); fulfillment changes allowed on cancelled/failed orders. MEDIUM: inventory never restored on refund/cancellation; duplicate shipping notifications; webhook secret in query params; plaintext shipping credentials; ship endpoint lacks status guard.

comments.author: mdbaldwin1
comments.text:
## Findings

### Finding 1 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/route.ts:143-144`
**Finding:** PATCH allows setting `status` to any value without transition validation. Cancelled orders can be resurrected to `paid`, paid orders regressed to `pending`. No terminal state enforcement.
**Recommendation:** Add transition map. `cancelled` and `failed` must be terminal states.

### Finding 2 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/route.ts:143-173`
**Finding:** No guard prevents fulfillment changes on cancelled/failed orders. Merchant can mark a cancelled order as `shipped` or `delivered`, triggering shipping notifications to the customer.
**Recommendation:** Only allow fulfillment changes when `order.status === "paid"`.

### Finding 3 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/route.ts:230-232`
**Finding:** Shipping notification sent whenever response shows `shipped`/`delivered`, even if status didn't change. Updating pickup fields on a shipped order re-sends notification.
**Recommendation:** Gate notification on actual status change: compare `currentOrder.fulfillment_status !== data.fulfillment_status`.

### Finding 4 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/refunds/[refundId]/route.ts`, `apps/web/lib/orders/refund-dispute-sync.ts`
**Finding:** Inventory is NEVER restored on refund. Checkout decrements inventory but no refund path restores it. Sold-out quantity permanently lost.
**Recommendation:** After successful refund, increment product_variants.inventory_qty for refunded items.

### Finding 5 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/route.ts:143-144`
**Finding:** Cancellation triggers only a notification email. No inventory restoration, no automatic Stripe refund, no fulfillment reset, no check that order isn't already shipped/delivered.
**Recommendation:** Cancellation should: guard against shipped/delivered orders, trigger refund, restore inventory.

### Finding 6 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/shipping/webhook/route.ts:14-27`
**Finding:** Webhook secret can be passed as query parameter when `SHIPPING_ALLOW_QUERY_TOKEN` enabled. Query params logged in access/CDN logs, leaking the secret.
**Recommendation:** Remove query-parameter auth path. Use headers only.

### Finding 7 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/shipping/store-config.ts:57-71`
**Finding:** Shipping API keys and webhook secrets stored in plaintext in `store_integrations`. DB compromise exposes all EasyPost credentials.
**Recommendation:** Encrypt at rest or use secrets manager.

### Finding 8 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/ship/route.ts:60`
**Finding:** Ship endpoint allows adding tracking to failed/cancelled orders with no `order.status` check. Would overwrite fulfillment_status and trigger shipping notification.
**Recommendation:** Add `order.status === "paid"` guard.

### Finding 9 — LOW
**Severity:** low
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/route.ts:124-199`
**Finding:** Fulfillment status read-then-write TOCTOU race. Concurrent requests could both validate against stale current status.
**Recommendation:** Use conditional UPDATE with `WHERE fulfillment_status = :expected`.

### Finding 10 — LOW
**Severity:** low
**Category:** gap
**Location:** All order routes
**Finding:** No bulk operations. No bulk-ship, bulk-fulfill, or bulk-status-update. High-volume stores forced to process one at a time.
**Recommendation:** Add batch endpoints.

### Finding 11 — LOW
**Severity:** low
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/[orderId]/route.ts`
**Finding:** No merchant notes field on orders. Only customer_note (set at checkout). No internal annotation capability.
**Recommendation:** Add merchant_notes field or order_notes table.

## Summary: 0 critical, 2 high, 6 medium, 3 low, 1 info

title: Phase 3: Promotions Engine Review
description:
Review promotion/discount system for correctness.

## Scope
- Discount types and application logic
- Stacking rules and mutual exclusion
- Redemption limits and expiration enforcement
- Promo code validation at checkout
- Preview/calculation accuracy
- Edge cases: expired mid-checkout, over-redemption, negative totals

## Acceptance Criteria
- Discount math is correct for all promo types
- Redemption limits enforced atomically
- Expired promos rejected at checkout time
- No path to negative order totals
notes: Review complete. 1 critical, 4 high, 3 medium, 2 low, 2 info. CRITICAL: discountCents hardcoded to 0 in Stripe finalization RPC fallback (maintenance hazard). HIGH: per-customer redemption limit unenforced in Stripe flow; hard-deleting promos breaks in-flight checkouts; modifying promo during in-flight sessions causes accounting mismatch; no validation cap on percent discounts or date ordering.

comments.author: mdbaldwin1
comments.text:
## Findings

### Finding 1 — CRITICAL
**Severity:** critical
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/storefront/checkout-finalization.ts:207`
**Finding:** In Stripe flow, `discountCents: 0` hardcoded when falling through to RPC. Current RPC recalculates from promo code so discount isn't lost, but if RPC ever changed to trust the param, all Stripe-flow discounts would silently zero out. Maintenance hazard.
**Recommendation:** Pass actual discount from checkout session data instead of hardcoding 0.

### Finding 2 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/storefront/checkout-finalization.ts:109-196`
**Finding:** When existing order found by paymentIntentId (Stripe flow), `promotion_redemptions` row is NEVER created and `times_redeemed` NEVER incremented. Per-customer redemption limit is effectively unenforced for all real Stripe payments — customers can reuse promo codes unlimited times.
**Recommendation:** After order update in existing-order branch, insert promotion_redemptions row and increment times_redeemed when checkout.promo_code is set.

### Finding 3 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/promotions/route.ts:192-229`
**Finding:** Promotion deletion is hard DELETE with ON DELETE CASCADE removing redemption records. In-flight Stripe sessions referencing the promo will fail at finalization — customer charged but order creation fails.
**Recommendation:** Use soft-delete (set is_active=false) or check for in-flight checkout sessions before allowing deletion.

### Finding 4 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/promotions/route.ts:139-189`
**Finding:** Promotion discount can be changed while Stripe sessions are in-flight. Stripe charged the old discount but RPC recalculates using new values — accounting mismatch between what customer paid and what order records.
**Recommendation:** Snapshot discount amount in storefront_checkout_sessions and use snapshot at finalization.

### Finding 5 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/promotions/route.ts:17-27`
**Finding:** No upper bound on percent discount — API accepts 500% or 99999%. Silently clamped to 100% at calculation time. Also no validation that startsAt < endsAt — can create promotions that can never be valid.
**Recommendation:** Add `.max(100)` for percent type. Add `.refine()` for startsAt < endsAt.

### Finding 6 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/promotions/preview/route.ts:59-64`
**Finding:** Preview doesn't check per_customer_redemption_limit (no customer context). Customer who exceeded limit sees "valid" preview, then gets rejected at checkout.
**Recommendation:** Accept optional email param, check redemption history when provided.

### Finding 7 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/checkout/route.ts:803`
**Finding:** `total_cents` stored on order does NOT include Stripe Tax. Tax exists only in Stripe. Order displays/exports understate actual amount charged.
**Recommendation:** Read tax-inclusive total from Stripe at finalization, or document total_cents as pre-tax.

### Finding 8 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `supabase/migrations/20260320230518:156`
**Finding:** RPC calculates platform fee but result is dead code — fee columns removed from order insert, value unused. Fee written separately by TypeScript.
**Recommendation:** Clean up dead fee calculation from RPC.

### Positive Findings
- Fixed-amount discount correctly capped at subtotal (prevents negative totals)
- Percent discount clamped to [1,100] at calculation time (3 redundant safety layers)
- Promo code lookup is case-insensitive (`.ilike`)
- Free shipping promos correctly zero out shipping fee
- $0 order total correctly rejected (Stripe can't process $0)

## Summary: 1 critical, 4 high, 3 medium, 2 low, 2 info

Review storefront rendering and platform billing.

comments.author: mdbaldwin1
comments.text:
## Findings

### Finding 1 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/s/[slug]/layout.tsx:16`, `apps/web/app/s/[slug]/page.tsx:36,74`
**Finding:** `loadStorefrontData(slug)` called 3 times per page view (layout metadata + page metadata + page render). With force-dynamic and no caching/dedup, each fires the full 8-query Promise.all. That's ~24+ DB queries per page view for identical data.
**Recommendation:** Wrap `loadStorefrontData` with React `cache()` for per-request dedup. Or load once in layout and pass via context.

### Finding 2 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/s/[slug]/page.tsx:83-100`
**Finding:** `buildStorefrontCanonicalUrl` called 5 separate times with await, each performing independent DB queries for domain resolution.
**Recommendation:** Compute canonical URL once at top of function, derive all schema URLs from it.

### Finding 3 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/storefront/load-storefront-data.ts:93`
**Finding:** Suspended stores show generic 404, same as non-existent stores. No distinct "suspended" message for returning customers. `unavailable.ts:55` explicitly excludes suspended from unavailable page flow.
**Recommendation:** Add suspended branch with distinct UI message.

### Finding 4 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/s/[slug]/products/[productId]/page.tsx:96`
**Finding:** Product JSON-LD hardcodes `priceCurrency: "USD"`. No reference to store currency.
**Recommendation:** Pull currency from store config.

### Finding 5 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/s/[slug]/products/[productId]/page.tsx:98`
**Finding:** Made-to-order variants with inventory_qty=0 marked "OutOfStock" in JSON-LD. Google shows "Out of stock" for purchasable items.
**Recommendation:** Check `is_made_to_order` and use PreOrder/InStock schema.

### Finding 6 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/storefront/metadata.ts:14,31-36`
**Finding:** Product pages don't include product images in OG tags. Social media shares show store's generic OG image instead of product photo.
**Recommendation:** Override openGraph.images with product's primary image on product pages.

### Finding 7 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/billing/fees.ts:36-37`
**Finding:** If no billing profile and default "standard" plan not found, fallback returns zero fees. Platform collects nothing — silent revenue leak with no logging.
**Recommendation:** Log warning or throw when default plan unresolvable.

### Finding 8 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/stores/payments/connect/route.ts:72`
**Finding:** Stripe Express account hardcodes `country: "US"`. International merchants cannot onboard.
**Recommendation:** Make configurable or document as known limitation.

### Finding 9 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/stripe/store-payments-readiness.ts:67`
**Finding:** `readyForLiveCheckout` requires Stripe Tax "active" even when merchant has `seller_attested_no_tax` decision. Blocks stores unnecessarily.
**Recommendation:** Bypass taxReady requirement when tax_collection_mode is seller_attested_no_tax.

### Finding 10 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/billing/reporting.ts:80`
**Finding:** Billing reconciliation compares fee_basis with order total, but discount timing can cause false positive mismatches.
**Recommendation:** Store fee basis independently, compare like-for-like.

### Finding 11 — LOW (x4)
**Severity:** low
**Category:** various
- LocalBusiness JSON-LD emitted for online-only stores (Google penalty risk)
- Domain TXT lookup fails silently for subdomains (resolveNs on non-apex)
- Domain deletion half-state if Resend removal fails after Vercel succeeds
- Inconsistent page width: loading-theme uses max-w-4xl, layout uses max-w-5xl

### Finding 12 — INFO
**Severity:** info
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/billing/` (all modules)
**Finding:** `monthly_price_cents` defined on BillingPlanOption but never charged. No subscription billing, grace periods, or overdue handling exists.
**Recommendation:** Implement or remove to avoid confusion.

title: Phase 4: API Consistency & Error Handling
description:

## Acceptance Criteria

title: Phase 4: Type Safety & Module Boundaries

title: Phase 5: Accessibility Review
description:
Review accessibility beyond automated axe-core checks.

## Scope

- Screen reader support (ARIA labels, live regions, semantic HTML)
- Focus management on route changes and modal open/close

comments.author: mdbaldwin1
comments.text:
## Findings

### Finding 1 — CRITICAL
**Severity:** critical
**Category:** security
**Location:** ~20 RLS policies across migrations (root cause: `20260316210000_store_lifecycle_workflow.sql`)
**Finding:** Store lifecycle migration renamed status `'active'` to `'live'` but ~20 RLS policies still check `s.status = 'active'`, which can NEVER match. Affected public_read/public_insert policies on: stores, products, store_settings, store_content_blocks, product_variants, product_option_axes, product_option_values, product_variant_option_values, store_experience_content, store_email_subscribers, store_legal_documents, store_privacy_profiles, store_privacy_requests, reviews, review_media, review_responses, review_aggregate_snapshots. This breaks all public storefront access via RLS. (Only works because service role bypasses RLS.)
**Recommendation:** P0 — Create migration updating all affected policies to use `s.status = 'live'`.

### Finding 2 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** security
**Location:** `20260320130000_stripe_webhook_event_ledger.sql:1-16`
**Finding:** `stripe_webhook_events` table has NO RLS at all. Contains Stripe event IDs, types, error messages. Any authenticated user with anon key could read all webhook events across all stores.
**Recommendation:** Enable RLS, restrict to service_role only.

### Finding 3 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** security
**Location:** `20260226193000_storefront_stripe_connect_checkout.sql:31`
**Finding:** `storefront_checkout_sessions` has RLS enabled but ZERO policies defined. Contains PII (customer_email, name, phone). No access possible via API — safe from leak perspective but may cause functionality gaps.
**Recommendation:** Add explicit SELECT policies for store members, or document as intentionally service-role-only.

### Finding 4 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** security
**Location:** `20260224123000_initial_schema.sql:153-168`
**Finding:** `store_branding` has only owner-all policy. No public read for storefront branding display. Works only via service role.
**Recommendation:** Add public SELECT policy scoped to live stores.

### Finding 5 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `20260307190000_billing_profile_backfill_and_api_rate_limits.sql:53-58`
**Finding:** `api_rate_limits` table has NO RLS. Any user could read/write rate limit buckets.
**Recommendation:** Enable RLS, restrict to service_role (used by security definer function).

### Finding 6 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `20260313114000_marketing_analytics_foundation.sql:77-80`
**Finding:** `marketing_sessions` and `marketing_events` have RLS enabled but zero policies. No access possible via API.
**Recommendation:** Add SELECT for platform admins, INSERT for anonymous if needed.

### Finding 7 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `20260314013000_accessibility_reports_foundation.sql:38`
**Finding:** `accessibility_reports` has RLS enabled but zero policies. Contains reporter PII.
**Recommendation:** Add public INSERT and admin SELECT policies.

### Finding 8 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `20260303234500_platform_roles_pickup_foundation.sql:247-349`
**Finding:** Pickup tables (pickup_locations, pickup_location_hours, pickup_blackout_dates) have management policies but no public read. Storefront customers can't see pickup options via API.
**Recommendation:** Add public SELECT for active locations at live stores.

### Finding 9 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** All migration files
**Finding:** No Supabase storage bucket RLS policies exist in any migration. Storage access control not version-controlled.
**Recommendation:** Define bucket policies in migrations for product images, branding, review media.

### Finding 10 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `20260227152000_store_shipping_integrations.sql:4-5`
**Finding:** `store_integrations` stores `shipping_api_key` and `shipping_webhook_secret` in plaintext. If DB compromised, all API keys exposed.
**Recommendation:** Encrypt with pgcrypto or move to secrets manager.

### Finding 11 — LOW
**Severity:** low
**Category:** security
**Location:** `20260224123000_initial_schema.sql:250-252`
**Finding:** `store_domains` public_verified_read doesn't check store status — exposes verified domains for suspended/removed stores.
**Recommendation:** Add store status check to policy.

### Finding 12 — LOW
**Severity:** low
**Category:** security
**Location:** Orders table policies
**Finding:** No customer self-read policy on orders. Customers can't view their own orders via API. Handled server-side via service role.
**Recommendation:** Consider adding customer self-read or document as intentional.

### Positive Findings
- Service role client properly server-side only (confirmed across ~150 files)
- User-scoped tables (profiles, memberships, customer tables) have correct self-read/write policies
- Platform admin tables properly restrict to admin/support roles
- Notification tables correctly scoped to recipients
- Audit events restricted to owner read-only

comments.author: mdbaldwin1
comments.text:
## Findings

### Finding 1 — CRITICAL
**Severity:** critical
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/stripe/webhooks/route.ts:15-17`
**Finding:** When `STRIPE_STUB_MODE=true`, the webhook endpoint returns `{ received: true, mode: "stub" }` immediately, bypassing ALL signature verification and event processing. If accidentally enabled in production, real Stripe payments succeed but orders are never created. Customers charged with no order.
**Recommendation:** Add hard guard: `if (isStripeStubMode() && process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production') throw new Error(...)`. Or check `VERCEL_ENV === 'production'`.

### Finding 2 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `supabase/migrations/20260320225222_storefront_checkout_billing_profile_fee_lookup.sql:85-110,206-226`
**Finding:** `stub_checkout_create_paid_order` RPC operates on `products` table (price_cents, inventory_qty) but checkout route resolves to `product_variants`. Inventory deducted from wrong table; prices from wrong source; order_items have no variant_id. Affects both stub mode AND webhook finalization fallback path.
**Recommendation:** Update RPC to operate on `product_variants` using the `variantId` already in the items payload.

### Finding 3 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/checkout/route.ts:813-900`
**Finding:** Stub mode creates fully `paid` orders without payment. No environment-level enforcement prevents this in production — relies entirely on operator discipline.
**Recommendation:** Same as Finding 1 — add hard production guard for stub mode.

### Finding 4 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/stripe/webhook-events.ts:36-80`
**Finding:** Webhook idempotency stale-event reclaim has TOCTOU race. Two retries hitting a stale event could both UPDATE and both proceed to process. No atomic compare-and-swap on the reclaim.
**Recommendation:** Add `.eq("status", "processing").lt("last_attempt_at", staleThreshold)` to reclaim UPDATE and check returned row count.

### Finding 5 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/checkout/route.ts:658-703`
**Finding:** Inventory availability checked at checkout creation but not reserved. Between check and webhook-driven deduction, another checkout can claim same inventory. RPC's FOR UPDATE lock only protects concurrent RPCs, not the gap.
**Recommendation:** Partially mitigated by RPC raising exception on insufficient inventory. Consider inventory reservation at checkout creation or ensure RPC error surfaces gracefully.

### Finding 6 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/stripe/webhooks/route.ts:31`
**Finding:** Webhook error response leaks raw Stripe error message to callers.
**Recommendation:** Return generic error, log details server-side.

### Finding 7 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/storefront/checkout-finalization.ts:246-248`
**Finding:** Platform fee recorded from checkout-time snapshot. If fee profile changes between checkout creation and payment, internal records may diverge from what Stripe actually collected.
**Recommendation:** Read actual `application_fee_amount` from Stripe PaymentIntent after finalization.

### Finding 8 — LOW
**Severity:** low
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/stores/payments/connect/route.ts:71-91`
**Finding:** If Stripe account creation succeeds but DB update fails, dangling Stripe account exists with no cleanup.
**Recommendation:** Add cleanup or use idempotency key for safe retry.

### Positive Findings
- Server-side price verification correct — client only submits IDs and quantities
- Multi-layered deduplication (webhook_events table + checkout session status + existing order check)
- Stripe Connect destination charges correctly configured with atomic application fees
- Fee calculation server-authoritative with proper clamping
- No subscription lifecycle webhooks (noted as gap if subscriptions planned)

comments.author: mdbaldwin1
comments.text:
## Findings

### Finding 1
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/platform/users/[userId]/role/route.ts:27`
**Finding:** `userId` path param used directly in Supabase query without Zod validation. Other routes correctly validate path params with `z.string().uuid()`.
**Recommendation:** Add Zod UUID validation for consistency.

### Finding 2
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/help/track/route.ts:13`
**Finding:** POST route inserts audit_events but does not call `enforceTrustedOrigin(request)`. Missing CSRF protection.
**Recommendation:** Add `enforceTrustedOrigin(request)`.

### Finding 3
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/legal/consent/route.ts:15`
**Finding:** POST route records legal acceptances without `enforceTrustedOrigin()`. CSRF could force logged-in user to accept legal terms.
**Recommendation:** Add `enforceTrustedOrigin(request)`.

### Finding 4
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/security/request-origin.ts:27-33`
**Finding:** `resolveAllowedHosts` includes `x-forwarded-host` in allowed origins. If attacker controls this header, they can match both Origin and allowed host to bypass the check. Safe on Vercel but widens trust surface.
**Recommendation:** In production, rely only on env-derived hosts (`NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL`, `VERCEL_PROJECT_PRODUCTION_URL`, `VERCEL_URL`), not request-supplied headers.

### Finding 5
**Severity:** low
**Category:** security
**Location:** Multiple upload routes (products/image, user/avatar, stores/branding/logo, rich-text/images)
**Finding:** SVG uploads accepted (`image/svg+xml`). SVGs can contain embedded JavaScript and could execute XSS if served inline from same origin.
**Recommendation:** Disallow SVG uploads, or sanitize server-side, or serve with `Content-Disposition: attachment`.

### Finding 6
**Severity:** low
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/stores/settings/route.ts:47`
**Finding:** `storefrontCopy` accepts `z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())` — any JSON structure stored. Low risk since React auto-escapes in JSX.
**Recommendation:** Tighten to `z.record(z.string(), z.string().max(500))`.

### Finding 7
**Severity:** info
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/rich-text.ts:300-315`
**Finding:** HTML sanitizer is custom regex-based rather than using DOMPurify. Implementation appears thorough (allowlisted tags, javascript: blocked, event handlers stripped) but regex sanitizers historically prone to bypasses.
**Recommendation:** Consider supplementing with DOMPurify for defense-in-depth, or add comprehensive XSS bypass test suite.

### Positive Findings
- Nearly every API route uses Zod with `parseJsonRequest()` for body validation
- No SQL injection risk — all queries use parameterized Supabase query builder
- All `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` usages properly sanitized or use JSON.stringify
- Open redirect prevention comprehensive via `sanitizeReturnTo()` with allowlisted prefixes
- File uploads use server-generated UUIDs for paths (no path traversal), enforce MIME and size limits
- CSRF protection consistent across mutation routes (2 exceptions noted)

comments.author: mdbaldwin1
comments.text:
## Findings

### Finding 1 — CRITICAL
**Severity:** critical
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `20260224123000_initial_schema.sql:15`
**Finding:** `stores.owner_user_id` uses `ON DELETE CASCADE`. Deleting an auth user CASCADES to destroy ALL their stores and everything downstream (orders, products, inventory, promotions, reviews, etc.). This is catastrophic data loss from a single user deletion.
**Recommendation:** Change to `ON DELETE RESTRICT` or `ON DELETE SET NULL`. Store data must survive user account deletion.

### Finding 2 — CRITICAL
**Severity:** critical
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `20260307143000_reviews_foundation.sql:69`
**Finding:** `review_aggregate_snapshots` has `PRIMARY KEY (store_id, product_id)` but `product_id` is nullable (references products with no NOT NULL). PostgreSQL does not allow NULL in primary keys — store-level aggregate inserts (product_id IS NULL) will fail.
**Recommendation:** Use sentinel UUID `'00000000-...'` for store-level aggregates, or use a unique index instead of PK.

### Finding 3 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `20260320225222_storefront_checkout_billing_profile_fee_lookup.sql:58`
**Finding:** RPC references `sbp.fee_override_bps` and `sbp.fee_override_fixed_cents` columns that were dropped in an earlier migration. Function would fail at runtime.
**Recommendation:** Fixed in next migration, but these should have been a single migration. Verify final state is correct.

### Finding 4 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** Multiple migrations — RPC rewritten 12+ times
**Finding:** `stub_checkout_create_paid_order` was rewritten 12+ times, ping-ponging between variant-aware and non-variant-aware, between different fee models. Final version may reference `platform_fee_bps`/`platform_fee_cents` columns that were dropped and may not be re-added.
**Recommendation:** Verify final schema state. Pin the RPC to a single well-tested version.

### Finding 5 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** performance
**Location:** `20260224123000_initial_schema.sql`
**Finding:** Missing indexes on `orders.customer_email` and `orders.status` despite heavy query usage.
**Recommendation:** Add indexes for these frequently-queried columns.

### Finding 6 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** performance
**Location:** `20260226193000_storefront_stripe_connect_checkout.sql`
**Finding:** `storefront_checkout_sessions` has no index on `order_id` (FK without index).
**Recommendation:** Add index on checkout_sessions(order_id).

### Finding 7 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `20260227120000_single_store_schema_cleanup.sql:3-10`
**Finding:** Destructive DROP CASCADE on 4 tables + DROP of financial columns (platform_fee_bps/cents) from orders. Later migrations re-add tables/columns, creating gap where historical data is lost.
**Recommendation:** Financial columns should never be dropped without verified data migration.

### Finding 8 — MEDIUM (x5)
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** various
**Locations:**
- `store_settings` table — 30+ columns, "junk drawer" pattern. Should decompose.
- `store_experience_content` — duplicates data from settings/branding/content_blocks, creates update anomalies.
- `store_integrations` — stores shipping API keys in plaintext.
- `orders.fulfillment_method` allows NULL without documented reason.
- `search_path` includes `auth` unnecessarily in membership policy helper function.

### Finding 9 — MEDIUM (indexes)
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** performance
**Location:** Various migrations
**Finding:** Missing composite indexes on: billing_events(store_id, occurred_at), legal_acceptances(legal_document_id).
**Recommendation:** Add targeted indexes for common query patterns.

### Finding 10 — LOW
**Severity:** low
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `20260303175500_enable_newsletter_defaults.sql:18`
**Finding:** UPDATE overwrites ALL store_settings rows with email_capture_enabled=true, even merchants who explicitly disabled it.
**Recommendation:** Add WHERE clause to only update unconfigured rows.

### Positive Findings
- Consistent UUID PKs, timestamptz, integer cents for money
- SECURITY DEFINER functions all set search_path correctly
- Good CHECK constraints on enum-like fields
- updated_at triggers consistently applied
- Foreign keys present on all expected relationships

## Findings

**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `supabase/migrations/20260320230518_storefront_checkout_orders_current_columns.sql:85-222`
**Finding:** Latest `stub_checkout_create_paid_order` RPC REGRESSED from variant-level to product-level inventory. It only uses `products.inventory_qty`, inserts order_items without `product_variant_id`/`variant_label`/`variant_snapshot`, records inventory_movements without variant_id. The earlier migration (20260302143000) had correct variant-level logic with made-to-order support. Current checkouts deduct from wrong level, lose variant traceability, break made-to-order.
**Recommendation:** Restore variant-level logic: SELECT FOR UPDATE on product_variants, decrement variant inventory, roll up to products, insert variant fields into order_items, respect is_made_to_order.

### Finding 2 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/inventory/adjust/route.ts:99-178`
**Finding:** Classic read-then-write race condition. Reads inventory_qty, computes nextInventory in JS, checks >= 0, writes back. Two concurrent adjustments can both read same value, both pass check, last write wins — one adjustment silently lost.
**Recommendation:** Use atomic SQL: `UPDATE product_variants SET inventory_qty = inventory_qty + $delta WHERE id = $id AND inventory_qty + $delta >= 0`. Check affected row count.

### Finding 3 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/checkout/route.ts:686-801`, `apps/web/lib/storefront/checkout-finalization.ts:199-210`
**Finding:** Promotion redemption limits checked at checkout creation but not locked. Between checkout and webhook finalization, another customer can exhaust the promo. RPC re-checks with FOR UPDATE, but if promo exhausted, customer is charged via Stripe with no order created — checkout just marked failed.
**Recommendation:** Handle exhausted-promo RPC exception gracefully — either create order without discount or trigger automatic Stripe refund.

### Finding 4 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/checkout-status/route.ts:54-71`, `apps/web/app/api/stripe/webhooks/route.ts:40-56`
**Finding:** Double finalization race between checkout-status polling and Stripe webhook. Both call `finalizeStorefrontCheckout()` concurrently. Both can read status="pending" and proceed. Unique index on stripe_payment_intent_id prevents duplicate orders, but inventory could be deducted twice (first commits, second fails).
**Recommendation:** Use atomic `UPDATE storefront_checkout_sessions SET status = 'processing' WHERE id = $id AND status = 'pending' RETURNING *` so only one caller wins.

### Finding 5 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/inventory/adjust/route.ts:173-226`
**Finding:** Inventory adjustment performs 4 non-transactional writes: update variant, re-aggregate product, insert movement, log audit. Crash mid-sequence leaves partial state.
**Recommendation:** Wrap steps 1-3 in a PL/pgSQL RPC for atomicity.

### Finding 6 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/storefront/checkout-finalization.ts:250-306`
**Finding:** After atomic RPC, follow-up writes (metadata, checkout status, fee breakdown, email) are non-transactional. Crash after RPC but before marking checkout "completed" causes retry to fail on unique constraint (Stripe path handled, stub path gets 500).
**Recommendation:** Mark checkout "completed" immediately after RPC, before enrichment.

### Finding 7 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/route.ts:143-163`
**Finding:** Order fulfillment_status transitions enforced only in application code, not DB. Direct DB access could set arbitrary statuses.
**Recommendation:** Add DB trigger for defense-in-depth (low priority if all mutations go through API).

### Finding 8 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/route.ts:143-145`
**Finding:** Order payment status has NO transition validation. Merchant could set cancelled→paid or paid→pending.
**Recommendation:** Add transition map for payment status. Prevent transitions from terminal states.

### Finding 9 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/shipping/webhook/route.ts:100-264`
**Finding:** Shipping webhook has NO idempotency mechanism (unlike Stripe webhook). Retries re-process tracking updates and re-send notification emails, potentially spamming customers.
**Recommendation:** Add idempotency table or deduplicate on trackerId+status. Skip notifications if status didn't change.

### Finding 10 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/refunds/route.ts:99-117`
**Finding:** Refundable balance has TOCTOU race. Two concurrent refund requests both read existing refunds, both pass check, both insert — potentially exceeding order total.
**Recommendation:** Use DB constraint or advisory lock to enforce refund total <= order total.

### Positive Findings
- Core RPC architecture is sound — single PL/pgSQL transaction with FOR UPDATE locking
- CHECK constraints on inventory_qty >= 0 provide DB-level safety net
- Stripe webhook idempotency well-designed with multi-layer dedup
- Fulfillment status has monotonic enforcement in shipping webhook via resolveMonotonicFulfillmentStatus

title: Phase 2: Query Performance & Pagination
description:
Review query patterns for performance issues.

## Scope
- N+1 query patterns in data loading
- Missing indexes on frequently queried columns
- Unbounded queries (known pagination gap)
- Large payload responses
- Supabase query builder usage patterns

## Acceptance Criteria
- No unbounded list queries in production paths
- All list endpoints have pagination or sensible limits
- No N+1 patterns in critical paths
- Index coverage adequate for query patterns
notes: Review complete. 4 critical, 7 high, 5 medium, 2 low, 2 info. 14 unbounded queries found, only 3 endpoints paginated. CRITICAL: orders, products, order export all unbounded; N+1 in weekly digest. HIGH: storefront has 10+ queries with zero caching; storefront products unbounded; multiple dashboard queries unbounded; missing indexes on key columns.

## Findings

**Severity:** critical

**Finding:** Orders export fetches ALL orders into memory for CSV. No limit, no streaming, no date filter.

### Finding 4 — CRITICAL
**Severity:** critical
**Category:** performance
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/notifications/digest/weekly.ts:169-205`
**Finding:** N+1 pattern: iterates ALL stores sequentially, 4+ DB queries per store per digest run. 100 stores = 400+ sequential queries.

### Finding 5 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** performance

### Finding 6 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** performance
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/storefront/load-storefront-data.ts:45-382`
**Finding:** Storefront page load requires 10+ queries in two waterfall stages. Zero caching (no unstable_cache, no revalidate, no in-memory cache). Every page view hits DB fresh.
**Recommendation:** Implement caching for infrequently-changing data (branding, settings, content blocks). Consider consolidating into a DB view.

### Finding 7 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** performance
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/storefront/load-storefront-data.ts:134-142`
**Finding:** Storefront loads ALL products with full nested variants/options. No pagination.

### Finding 8 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** performance
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/dashboard/store-hub/get-store-hub-data.ts:246-320`
**Finding:** Store Hub fires 12 parallel queries, several unbounded (products, subscribers, promotions, reviews).
**Recommendation:** Add limits. Use count queries instead of fetching all rows for reviews/subscribers.

### Finding 9 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** performance
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/dashboard/store-dashboard/get-store-dashboard-data.ts:271-279`
**Finding:** Performance orders limited to 1000 with nested joins. `previousPerformanceOrders` has NO limit.

### Finding 10 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** performance
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/dashboard/home/get-dashboard-home-data.ts:217-297`
**Finding:** Customer saved stores/items and managed store orders all unbounded.
**Recommendation:** Add `.limit()` to all three queries.

### Finding 11 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** performance
**Location:** Multiple files using `.ilike("customer_email", ...)`
**Finding:** ILIKE on customer_email can't use B-tree index. No index on orders.customer_email in migrations.
**Recommendation:** Create functional index on `lower(customer_email)`, use `.eq()` with normalized email.

### Finding 12 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** performance
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/platform/users/route.ts:40`
**Finding:** Fetches ALL store_memberships with no filter. Grows with every member added platform-wide.

**Severity:** medium
**Category:** performance
**Location:** Migrations
**Finding:** Missing indexes on: orders(customer_email), orders(store_id, created_at), store_memberships(user_id, status), store_memberships(store_id, status), notifications(recipient_user_id, read_at), store_settings(store_id).

### Positive Findings
- Notifications API has proper offset-based pagination with `.range()` — good template
- Reviews module implements proper pagination with multiple sort orders
- Most queries use Promise.all for parallel execution
- Explicit column selection in most queries

title: Phase 3: Checkout & Payment Flow Review
description:
End-to-end review of the checkout and payment flow.

## Scope
- Cart management → checkout session creation
- Stripe checkout session → payment confirmation
- Order creation with inventory deduction
- Application fee and platform fee calculation
- Tax calculation (Stripe Tax integration)
- Error handling and recovery (failed payments, expired sessions)
- Webhook-driven order finalization

## Acceptance Criteria
- Complete happy path verified
- All failure modes handled gracefully
- No orphaned checkout sessions or phantom orders
- Fee math verified against documented rates
notes: Review complete. 0 critical, 2 high, 5 medium, 3 low, 1 info. HIGH: pickup fee silently dropped from Stripe session (revenue shortfall); no auto-refund when store goes offline after payment (orphaned payment). MEDIUM: cart preview shows draft/archived items; checkout-status leaks error details; platform fee on pre-tax only; no expired session handler; unbounded items array.

comments.author: mdbaldwin1
comments.text:
## Findings

### Finding 1 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/checkout/route.ts:1014-1037`
**Finding:** Pickup fee silently dropped from Stripe session. When fulfillment is `pickup`, shipping_options omitted but app records shippingFeeCents from pickup config. Stripe collects less than system records as total_cents. Revenue shortfall for stores with non-zero pickup fees.
**Recommendation:** Add pickup fee as shipping_options entry or additional line item in Stripe session.

### Finding 2 — HIGH
**Severity:** high
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/storefront/checkout-finalization.ts:104-106`
**Finding:** When store goes offline after payment, checkout marked "failed" but NO Stripe refund issued. Customer charged with no order and no automatic refund — orphaned payment requiring manual intervention.
**Recommendation:** Auto-initiate refund when marking checkout failed after successful payment. Or queue for admin review.

### Finding 3 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/storefront/cart-preview/route.ts:61-72`
**Finding:** Cart preview doesn't filter by product/variant status. Shows prices for unpublished/archived/draft items, misleading customers.
**Recommendation:** Add `.eq("status", "active")` to queries, matching checkout route validation.

### Finding 4 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** security
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/checkout-status/route.ts:75`
**Finding:** Checkout-status catches all errors and returns raw error message to unauthenticated users. Leaks Supabase/Stripe internal details.
**Recommendation:** Return generic error, log details server-side.

### Finding 5 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/checkout/route.ts:806-807`
**Finding:** Platform fee calculated on pre-tax total. When Stripe automatic_tax adds tax, actual charge is higher but application_fee_amount was pre-computed. Platform fee basis doesn't account for tax.
**Recommendation:** Clarify pre-tax vs post-tax fee policy. If post-tax intended, adjust after session completes.

### Finding 6 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/stripe/webhooks/route.ts:59-69`
**Finding:** No handler for `checkout.session.expired` events. Expired sessions leave storefront_checkout_sessions in `pending` forever. Stale records accumulate with no cleanup.
**Recommendation:** Handle expired event to mark as failed. Or implement periodic cleanup job.

### Finding 7 — MEDIUM
**Severity:** medium
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/checkout/route.ts:26-34`
**Finding:** Checkout items array has no `.max()` bound (cart-preview has `.max(24)`). Could submit hundreds of items in single checkout.
**Recommendation:** Add `.max(24)` to items array in checkout payloadSchema.

### Finding 8 — LOW
**Severity:** low
**Category:** gap
**Location:** `apps/web/lib/notifications/order-emails.ts:374-393`
**Finding:** Email send failures silently swallowed. No retry queue. If email provider down at order creation, both customer and merchant notifications permanently lost.
**Recommendation:** Add async retry queue or cron sweep for undelivered order notifications.

### Finding 9-10 — LOW
**Severity:** low
**Category:** correctness
**Location:** `apps/web/app/api/orders/checkout/route.ts:1017,98`
**Finding:** Shipping countries hardcoded to US only. Currency hardcoded to USD. Product limitations — not bugs but undocumented constraints.
**Recommendation:** Make configurable per store or document as known limitations.

Transform /pricing into a decision page with plan comparison matrix, transparent fee explanation, FAQs, and trust messaging to reduce purchase anxiety.

Acceptance criteria:
- Plan matrix accuracy validated against billing config
- FAQ addresses common objections
- CTA paths to signup/contact measured

title: SEO landing pages + content growth baseline
description:
Add high-intent SEO landing pages and content templates for acquisition queries; include technical SEO hardening and internal linking strategy.

Acceptance criteria:
- Initial keyword-targeted pages live
- Sitemap/internal links updated
- Search console measurement plan documented

title: Marketing analytics + CRO experimentation loop
description:
Implement marketing event instrumentation and a lightweight experiment framework for iterative conversion optimization.

Acceptance criteria:
- Funnel events tracked from visit to signup
- Dashboard/reporting for conversion by page and CTA
- Experiment process documented

title: Legal documents and version tables
description:
Create legal document/version data model: legal_documents, legal_document_versions, and legal_acceptances with immutable acceptance evidence fields (timestamp, user, version hash, surface).

Acceptance criteria:
- Migration + RLS policies implemented
- Version publishing model documented
- Acceptance records immutable by policy

title: Signup acceptance capture (terms + privacy)
description:
Add mandatory legal acceptance controls to signup with explicit consent language and stored version identifiers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Signup blocked unless required boxes checked
- Acceptance rows created with current version ids
- UI links to active legal docs

title: Re-consent gate for updated required legal versions
description:
Implement middleware/app gate requiring users to accept newly required legal versions before continuing to app routes.

Acceptance criteria:
- Users with stale consent are redirected to consent screen
- Acceptance completion returns user to intended path
- Tests cover gating and bypass rules

title: Admin legal publishing and acceptance audit UI
description:
Build admin/legal management UI to create/publish legal versions and inspect acceptance records for compliance/audit response.

Acceptance criteria:
- Admin can publish new versions with effective dates
- Acceptance lookup by user/store/version works
- Export/report capability available

title: Legal communication + operational runbook
description:
Define and implement communication mechanics for material legal updates (in-app + email), plus incident/runbook guidance for legal disputes and evidence requests.

Acceptance criteria:
- Notification templates for legal update messages
- Runbook added under docs/runbooks
- Owner/admin guidance documented

title: Docs platform migration (from in-code static to content-driven)
description:
Migrate docs content from hardcoded TypeScript structures to content-driven markdown/MDX with typed frontmatter and rendering pipeline.

Acceptance criteria:
- Existing docs pages render from content files
- Category/nav metadata preserved or improved
- Tests validate content schema and route generation

title: Harden avatar lifecycle (staging, cleanup, discard/save semantics)
description: Ensure avatar uploads cannot be silently lost or orphaned. Preserve clear save/discard semantics, clean up temporary uploads, and add robust API failure handling paths.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to develop via PRs #64-#68.

title: Merchant docs expansion
description:
Author complete merchant-facing docs for onboarding, catalog, orders, pickup/shipping, domains, emails, reviews, analytics, and troubleshooting.

Acceptance criteria:
- Each critical merchant workflow has a doc
- Cross-links and prerequisites included
- Reviewed for factual consistency with current UI

title: Admin/operator docs expansion
description:
Create admin/operator docs for moderation, approvals, governance, incident response, deployment/release operations, and support playbooks.

Acceptance criteria:
- Admin workspace pages all have supporting docs
- Incident and escalation paths are explicit
- Runbooks reference real commands/routes

title: In-app contextual help integration
description:
Add context-aware help entry points from key dashboard/store settings/workflow screens into relevant docs pages.

Acceptance criteria:
- Help links present on high-friction screens
- Links deep-link to specific docs anchors/sections
- Basic usage telemetry captured

title: Docs governance: ownership, freshness SLAs, and release checklist integration
description:
Establish docs ownership and freshness process, and integrate docs updates into PR/release readiness standards.

Acceptance criteria:
- Doc owner + review cadence metadata exists
- Release checklist includes docs verification gates
- Stale-doc detection process documented

title: Store workspace overhaul epic
description: Rebuild the store settings, content workspace, and Storefront Studio into a coherent, store-scoped editing system with shared editor infrastructure, robust preview fidelity, and significantly improved UX. This epic covers architecture cleanup, duplicate route retirement, shared config/editor abstractions, Studio redesign, settings IA consolidation, and verification for multi-store editing flows.

title: Storefront Studio redesign
description: Rebuild Storefront Studio into a primary editing experience with a coherent surface navigator, inline inspector controls, accurate preview modes, publish state, and validation feedback. The Studio should stop acting as a wrapper that punts users into separate forms for most tasks.
close_reason: Completed Storefront Studio redesign hardening beads covering autosave integrity, canonical shell URL sync, accessibility semantics, route canonicalization cleanup, shell interaction polish, and regression coverage.

title: Fix shared accessibility semantics in Storefront Studio controls
description: Repair the systemic accessibility gaps in the refactored Studio controls. Ensure shared form labels are associated with inputs, switches expose accessible names and state, and collapsed rail/editor target interactions have clear semantics beyond visual context.
acceptance_criteria: Representative Studio fields have associated labels or equivalent accessible naming; switch/toggle controls use consistent accessible ids and semantics; collapsed rail/editor target interactions expose clear names and active state; accessibility-focused tests cover shared primitives or representative Studio panels.
close_reason: Bound shared Studio labels to controls, added explicit ids for switch-row interactions, and added primitive accessibility coverage for FormField.

title: Make Storefront Studio autosave race-safe
description: Fix the document-provider autosave model so in-flight section, store, branding, and settings saves cannot overwrite newer local edits. Add request/version guards or equivalent conflict handling, and keep draft + preview state stable while autosave is running.
acceptance_criteria: Typing during autosave does not lose newer edits when a response resolves; section autosave and store/branding/settings autosave each guard against stale response clobbering; errors do not reset newer draft state; regression coverage includes at least one overlapping-edit autosave scenario.
notes: Implemented race-safe autosave guards in `storefront-studio-document-provider` by preventing duplicate in-flight saves per slice, ignoring stale save responses, and only applying returned values to the live draft when no newer local edits exist. Added helper coverage in `tests/storefront-draft.test.ts`. Validation: `npm run typecheck`, `npm test -- --run tests/storefront-draft.test.ts`.
close_reason: Implemented race-safe autosave guards and added focused draft reconciliation coverage.

title: Synchronize Storefront Studio shell navigation with canonical surface URLs
description: Keep the Studio shell deep-linkable and navigable. Surface changes from the rail and preview should update the canonical `?surface=` URL, support browser back/forward, and preserve the selected surface across reloads.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting a page surface updates the canonical Studio URL; browser back/forward restores the expected surface; reload preserves the selected surface; redirect tests and shell interaction tests cover the URL contract.
close_reason: Synchronized Studio page-surface navigation with canonical surface URLs and added helper coverage for URL generation and href mapping.

title: Finish Storefront Studio route canonicalization and residual legacy cleanup
description: Validate the redirect-based retirement work already in progress. Remove any remaining dashboard nav or wrapper behaviors that expose deprecated content-workspace/content-studio entry points, and keep Email Studio handoff clean for email routes.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard nav exposes only canonical Studio entry points; retired storefront content routes redirect intentionally to Storefront Studio or Email Studio; no residual page-only affordances are stranded on deprecated wrappers; focused route tests cover the final redirect matrix.
close_reason: Aligned legacy storefront-content redirects with the canonical Studio home URL, trimmed stale back-button handling, and expanded route canonicalization coverage.

title: Constrain Storefront Studio shell side effects and interaction polish
description: Review the new shell behavior for broader dashboard side effects and interaction rough edges. Make Studio-specific sidebar collapse behavior explicit and reversible, validate fullscreen/preview behavior, and smooth remaining responsive interaction issues in the left rail and preview canvas.
acceptance_criteria: Studio-specific shell state changes are intentional and do not silently leave the wider dashboard in an unexpected state; fullscreen preview and responsive rail behavior are predictable on common desktop/laptop sizes; focused tests or QA notes cover the finalized interaction behavior.
close_reason: Made Studio sidebar collapse temporary, added fullscreen keyboard/modal semantics, and improved control state semantics for preview mode and collapsed rail interactions.

title: Backfill regression coverage for the new Storefront Studio architecture
description: Add coverage for the document-provider-driven Studio after the hardening work lands: autosave integrity, canonical surface URL sync, redirect behavior, representative accessibility expectations, and shell interaction behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover autosave race safety, canonical surface routing, legacy redirect behavior, and at least one representative accessibility expectation for shared Studio controls; touched tests run as part of normal validation for the Studio area.

title: Store-scoped dashboard editor context
description: Fix store workspace correctness by ensuring all content workspace, Storefront Studio, preview, and store settings APIs resolve the target store from the slugged workspace context rather than the implicit active-store bundle. Unify store access checks for multi-store users and add tests covering cross-store read/write isolation.
notes: Implemented route-scoped store resolution across dashboard editor APIs and client callers. Added optional slug-aware store bundle resolution/auth helpers, scoped API URL helper, and regression coverage for content/settings/members routes. Validation: npm run -w @myrivo/web test -- store-experience-content-route.test.ts store-experience-settings-route.test.ts; npm run -w @myrivo/web typecheck. Local-only node_modules symlinks were used in the worktree for validation and are not committed.

title: Canonical content/settings route architecture
description: Collapse duplicate workspace entrypoints into one canonical route model. Content Workspace should have one stable landing route and content-studio should become compatibility redirects only. Store settings should have a clear overview-to-subsection navigation model with no competing legacy entrypoints.

title: Shared store editor infrastructure
description: Create shared schemas, path helpers, dirty-state handling, autosave/publish mechanics, validation, and assembled storefront config primitives for store content/settings editors. The goal is to eliminate repeated one-off form state and ensure Studio, content forms, and storefront rendering all consume the same typed configuration model.

title: Store settings IA and UX consolidation
description: Refactor store settings into clearer operational groupings and shared form patterns. Separate content, design, and operational configuration boundaries; reduce duplicated fetch/state logic; and improve save/discard/error UX across general, branding, checkout, shipping, pickup, domains, integrations, and team settings.

title: Stabilize generated typings and repository hygiene
description: Eliminate unstable generated-file churn (e.g., next-env dev route import), ensure deterministic typing file state, and add guardrails so local dev does not dirty develop unexpectedly.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to develop via PRs #64-#68.

title: Store workspace verification coverage
description: Add regression coverage for store-scoped editor APIs, draft save/publish flows, storefront preview fidelity, settings propagation into storefront renderers, and multi-store user scenarios. Include focused tests for known failure modes such as nested section value resolution and wrong-store writes.

title: Store workspace sidebar and Studio UX refinement
description: Refine the post-overhaul store workspace UX by replacing the three-pane Storefront Studio with a two-pane layout, introducing a shared collapsible sidebar model across dashboard workspaces, and improving responsive preview usability. This follow-on epic covers sidebar state/persistence, workspace shell standardization, Studio preview sizing, and regression coverage for desktop/mobile navigation behavior.

title: Shared collapsible dashboard sidebar shell
description: Build a shared dashboard sidebar shell with expanded, collapsed, and mobile drawer states. The shell should support a slim icon rail on desktop, a persistent collapse preference, and consistent rendering for store workspace, settings, reports, and Studio navigation.

title: Convert Storefront Studio to two-pane layout
description: Replace the current three-pane Storefront Studio with a slim left inspector/navigation rail and a dominant right preview pane. Remove the permanent right-side inspector, move guidance into collapsible sections, improve preview fit modes, and preserve inline editing inside the left rail.

title: Migrate store workspaces onto shared sidebar UX
description: Apply the shared sidebar shell and collapse behavior across content workspace, store settings, reports, and related store workspace sub-navigation. Ensure each workspace uses the same expand/collapse mental model and responsive drawer behavior.

title: Responsive preview and sidebar interaction polish
description: Refine viewport sizing, mobile/tablet behavior, transition details, keyboard affordances, and sidebar persistence so preview areas keep priority on constrained screens. This includes minimizing device chrome waste and ensuring drawers/sheets do not fight the main content area.

title: Sidebar and Studio UX regression coverage
description: Add focused tests for sidebar collapse persistence, workspace navigation rendering, Studio two-pane layout behavior, and responsive drawer/fallback states. Cover the main desktop and mobile UX paths so future layout changes do not regress preview usability.

title: Simplify Storefront Studio editor panel componentization
Make storefront surfaces mobile-first and tablet-strong
description: Rework the customer-facing storefront surfaces for phone- and tablet-first shopping. Focus on navigation, product browsing, product detail, cart, footer, and shared responsive layout behavior so storefronts feel intentional on small screens rather than collapsed desktop layouts.

title: Home and products mobile/tablet layout redesign
description: Rework the storefront home and products surfaces for mobile and tablet, including hero composition, filter controls, product-grid behavior, and smaller-screen control density.

title: Mobile storefront header navigation and top-of-page behavior
description: Add a real mobile navigation pattern for storefronts, reduce small-screen header height, and tune announcement/header interplay so the first viewport is usable on phones and tablets.

title: Shared storefront responsive layout primitives
description: Introduce shared responsive layout helpers or tokens for storefront spacing, horizontal padding, and split layouts so mobile and tablet treatments stay consistent across customer-facing pages.

title: Product detail page responsive redesign
description: Make the product detail experience mobile-first and tablet-strong by replacing rigid split layouts, improving media-content balance, and preserving strong CTA readability on small screens.

title: Cart and checkout-entry responsive redesign
description: Improve the cart experience for mobile and tablet by removing cramped side-rail behavior, improving summary placement, and making checkout-entry forms easier to scan and complete on small screens.

title: Footer mobile/tablet redesign
description: Redesign the storefront footer for smaller screens with intentional mobile composition, better hierarchy, and improved newsletter, support, and navigation grouping.

title: About and policies responsive polish
description: Refine the editorial storefront pages for mobile and tablet, including hero balance, content widths, FAQ and support grids, and image-text section behavior without regressing inline Studio editing.

title: Responsive hardening for storefront Studio inline affordances
description: Audit and improve inline Studio edit affordances on narrow screens so popovers, edit buttons, and inline controls stay usable on phone and tablet previews.

title: Responsive storefront verification and regression coverage
description: Add targeted regression coverage and perform structured verification for storefront responsive behavior across home, products, product detail, cart, checkout, about, policies, and footer surfaces.

title: Pickup order override workflow with customer notification
Add merchant pickup reschedule override UI in Orders
Design pickup override contract and lifecycle rules
Add pickup override API, validation, and audit trail
Send mandatory customer pickup-change notifications and emails
Backfill pickup override regression coverage
Refactor dashboard product manager into modular units
description: Break the oversized product manager component into focused hooks/components, remove hook-dependency suppressions, and preserve all current behaviors with tests.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to develop via PRs #64-#68.

title: Store-level legal documents for privacy and terms
description: Add store-level Privacy Policy and Terms & Conditions support with a merchant UX that is legally useful, operationally maintainable, and clearly separated from storefront presentation copy. The intended model is a Store Settings > Legal surface for formal documents, while Storefront Studio remains focused on customer-facing policy summaries, FAQs, and support messaging.

title: Define store legal information architecture and ownership split
description: Define the UX and ownership split for store-level legal documents. Decide what belongs in Store Settings versus Storefront Studio, how merchants discover and manage Privacy Policy and Terms, and what the initial non-goals are so we avoid overbuilding a full Legal Studio too early.

title: Add store legal document data model and template resolution
description: Add the underlying data model and template-resolution layer for store-level Privacy Policy and Terms documents. This should support strong defaults, merchant-specific variables, and a clean runtime shape without forcing merchants to author legal text from scratch.

title: Build Store Settings legal editor for privacy and terms
description: Build a dedicated Store Settings legal editor for Privacy Policy and Terms & Conditions. The UX should favor clarity and trust over visual novelty, with a strong editing experience, preview, and obvious publication state.

title: Render storefront privacy and terms pages from store legal documents
description: Render public storefront privacy and terms pages from store-level legal documents, with safe fallbacks and clean routing. These pages should feel like first-class storefront pages, not system leftovers.

title: Add template-backed merchant variables and preview for legal documents
description: Add a template-backed variable system and preview model so merchants can personalize core legal documents without freehand-editing every clause. This should balance power with guardrails and avoid a confusing block-builder experience.

title: Wire storefront navigation, footer links, and policy page references to legal docs
description: Wire storefront navigation, footer links, and policy-page references so customers can actually reach store-level Privacy Policy and Terms documents at the right moments. This includes aligning existing policy/support flows with the new legal-document destinations.

title: Add legal document publishing/version metadata and change safeguards
description: Add publishing metadata, effective-date behavior, and merchant safeguards for legal changes. The goal is to prevent accidental silent edits and make legal updates feel deliberate and auditable.

title: Backfill tests and rollout docs for store legal documents
description: Backfill tests, rollout documentation, and operator guidance for store-level legal documents so the feature is supportable after launch.

title: Privacy compliance layer: notice at collection, California rights, and merchant configuration
Add privacy compliance data model and configuration layer
description: Add the persistence and configuration model for privacy compliance so stores and the platform can resolve the correct notices, California addenda, contact fields, and publishing state without hardcoded page copy.

title: Define privacy compliance IA and platform/store ownership
description: Define the product boundary for privacy compliance across platform-managed legal requirements, store-configurable legal copy, point-of-collection notices, and California-specific rights/opt-out workflows. Document where each disclosure appears in the shopper journey and who owns the content.

title: Implement point-of-collection privacy notices across shopper entry points
description: Add privacy notice at collection support anywhere shopper data is collected, including checkout, newsletter signup, account creation, support/contact inputs, and other customer-facing forms that collect personal information.
close_reason: Completed with reusable collection notices wired into checkout, newsletter signup, and review submission.

title: Add California privacy rights and opt-out surfaces
description: Implement California-specific privacy compliance surfaces where applicable, including rights disclosure language, optional do-not-sell/share handling, and product hooks for future Global Privacy Control or rights-request expansion.

title: Build Store Settings privacy compliance editor and defaults
description: Build the merchant-facing settings surface for privacy compliance, including reusable defaults, configurable contact details, optional California-specific addenda, and clear guidance about what merchants are expected to customize versus what Myrivo manages centrally.

title: Render storefront privacy compliance surfaces and legal links
description: Render the correct privacy disclosures on the storefront, including privacy policy routing, California-specific notice surfaces where applicable, and links from the appropriate storefront pages and collection flows.

title: Add privacy request intake, auditability, and operator workflow hooks
description: Add the product hooks needed to capture privacy-related requests or opt-out actions, persist an auditable trail, and give support/operators a clear operational path even if full automated rights fulfillment comes later.
close_reason: Completed with shopper privacy request intake, owner/admin request status workflow, and audit hooks for request updates.

title: Backfill privacy compliance verification, rollout docs, and support guidance
description: Add tests, rollout notes, and support documentation for privacy compliance so the product layer is operationally supportable and legally safer to evolve after launch.

title: Standardize API request parsing and safe error responses
description: Harden API routes by adding safe JSON parsing and consistent error responses (especially around profile/store settings mutations) while preserving current behavior.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to develop via PRs #64-#68.

title: Cookie compliance layer: consent banner, preferences, and analytics gating
description: Add a platform-managed cookie compliance layer for public Myrivo surfaces and storefronts, including cookie inventory/policy, shopper-facing consent UX, and consent-gated non-essential analytics.

title: Define cookie inventory, categories, and ownership
description: Document the cookie and similar-technology inventory across public Myrivo surfaces and storefronts, classify cookies as essential vs analytics, define platform-vs-store ownership, and establish the UX/compliance boundary for the first rollout.

title: Build cookie policy page, consent storage, and public consent UX
description: Implement the cookie policy page plus a public cookie banner/preferences center that lets shoppers accept essential-only or analytics cookies and revisit their choice later.

title: Gate storefront analytics and non-essential storage by consent
description: Ensure storefront analytics cookies/local storage and analytics collection only run after the user has granted analytics consent, and add defensive server-side checks for the consent state.

title: Backfill cookie compliance tests, support docs, and rollout notes
description: Add regression coverage, support/runbook documentation, and changelog updates for the cookie compliance layer.

title: Refunds and disputes operations program
Add customer communications and policy-aware refund/dispute messaging
description: Send clear customer-facing communications for refunds and disputes, including transactional email/in-app messaging and policy-aware copy that aligns with store policy context. Acceptance criteria: customer messages are triggered for relevant refund/dispute events; copy is configurable where appropriate; support links/policy context are included.

title: Integrate Stripe refunds, dispute visibility, and order timeline synchronization
description: Wire refund execution to Stripe, add dashboard visibility into disputes and their statuses, and synchronize refund/dispute events into order state and timeline history. Acceptance criteria: refunds call Stripe correctly; disputes appear in merchant UI; order timeline reflects refund/dispute lifecycle changes.

title: Define refund/dispute ownership model, statuses, and UX contract
description: Define the end-to-end refunds and disputes model for Myrivo, including refund types, dispute visibility, merchant/staff responsibilities, customer communication expectations, and dashboard UX surfaces. Acceptance criteria: documented status model; refund/dispute UX contract captured in docs; implementation constraints and sequencing for follow-on work are explicit.

title: Build merchant refund actions and partial/full refund flows in order detail
description: Add owner/staff refund actions to the order detail experience, including full and partial refund flows, required reason capture, amount preview, confirmation UX, and order timeline updates. Acceptance criteria: refund actions exist in order detail/flyout; partial and full refund flows are usable; reasons and timeline state are persisted.
